  "text": "The Cephx authentication protocol implementation in Ceph v19.2.1 is distributed across src/auth/ and src/mon/ directories. The core protocol definitions are in src/auth/cephx/CephxProtocol.h, which defines message types including CephXServerChallenge, CephXAuthenticate, CephXSessionAuthInfo, CephXAuthorizer, and CephXAuthorizeChallenge. These structures implement ticket-based authentication with request/response headers (CephXRequestHeader, CephXResponseHeader) and support for rotating keys. The CephXTicketHandler and CephXTicketManager manage client-side ticket caching and lifecycle. The CephxClientHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxClientHandler.h/cc) implements the AuthClientHandler interface and is responsible for client-side authentication, including building authorizers via the build_authorizer() method and handling responses. The CephxServiceHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxServiceHandler.h/cc) implements the AuthServiceHandler interface for server-side authentication verification. CephxAuthorizeHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxAuthorizeHandler.h/cc) verifies authorizers sent in connection establishment. CephxSessionHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxSessionHandler.h/cc) handles signing and verification of session messages. The AuthAuthorizer interface (src/auth/Auth.h) is the abstract base for all protocol-specific authorizers. Key management is handled by KeyServer (src/auth/cephx/CephxKeyServer.h/cc) which manages ticket generation, maintains rotating keys, and implements build_session_auth_info(). RotatingKeyRing (src/auth/RotatingKeyRing.h/cc) manages client-side rotating secrets with methods like need_new_secrets() and set_secrets(). Key rotation logic is defined in KeyServer with the _rotate_secret() internal method and prepare_rotating_update() public method. MonClient (src/mon/MonClient.h/cc) manages client authentication to monitors with the handle_auth() method coordinating the authentication handshake. MonConnection handles individual monitor connections with authenticate() and handle_auth() managing the protocol state machine. AuthMonitor (src/mon/AuthMonitor.h/cc) serves as the server-side monitor authentication provider. The messaging layer uses MAuth (src/messages/MAuth.h) for client requests and MAuthReply (src/messages/MAuthReply.h) for server responses. Protocol message types are defined via macros: CEPHX_GET_AUTH_SESSION_KEY (0x0100), CEPHX_GET_PRINCIPAL_SESSION_KEY (0x0200), and CEPHX_GET_ROTATING_KEY (0x0400) in CephxProtocol.h. The authentication registry (src/auth/AuthRegistry.h/cc) manages available authentication methods and provides factory methods."
}

  "text": "The Cephx authentication protocol implementation in Ceph v19.2.1 is distributed across src/auth/ and src/mon/ directories. The core protocol definitions are in src/auth/cephx/CephxProtocol.h, which defines message types including CephXServerChallenge, CephXAuthenticate, CephXSessionAuthInfo, CephXAuthorizer, and CephXAuthorizeChallenge. These structures implement ticket-based authentication with request/response headers (CephXRequestHeader, CephXResponseHeader) and support for rotating keys. The CephXTicketHandler and CephXTicketManager manage client-side ticket caching and lifecycle. The CephxClientHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxClientHandler.h/cc) implements the AuthClientHandler interface and is responsible for client-side authentication, including building authorizers via the build_authorizer() method and handling responses. The CephxServiceHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxServiceHandler.h/cc) implements the AuthServiceHandler interface for server-side authentication verification. CephxAuthorizeHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxAuthorizeHandler.h/cc) verifies authorizers sent in connection establishment. CephxSessionHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxSessionHandler.h/cc) handles signing and verification of session messages. The AuthAuthorizer interface (src/auth/Auth.h) is the abstract base for all protocol-specific authorizers. Key management is handled by KeyServer (src/auth/cephx/CephxKeyServer.h/cc) which manages ticket generation, maintains rotating keys, and implements build_session_auth_info(). RotatingKeyRing (src/auth/RotatingKeyRing.h/cc) manages client-side rotating secrets with methods like need_new_secrets() and set_secrets(). Key rotation logic is defined in KeyServer with the _rotate_secret() internal method and prepare_rotating_update() public method. MonClient (src/mon/MonClient.h/cc) manages client authentication to monitors with the handle_auth() method coordinating the authentication handshake. MonConnection handles individual monitor connections with authenticate() and handle_auth() managing the protocol state machine. AuthMonitor (src/mon/AuthMonitor.h/cc) serves as the server-side monitor authentication provider. The messaging layer uses MAuth (src/messages/MAuth.h) for client requests and MAuthReply (src/messages/MAuthReply.h) for server responses. Protocol message types are defined via macros: CEPHX_GET_AUTH_SESSION_KEY (0x0100), CEPHX_GET_PRINCIPAL_SESSION_KEY (0x0200), and CEPHX_GET_ROTATING_KEY (0x0400) in CephxProtocol.h. The authentication registry (src/auth/AuthRegistry.h/cc) manages available authentication methods and provides factory methods."
}
